blocks.content: They stayed longer than either of them planned.
June looked up from her book at some point and watched them with the quiet satisfaction of someone whose space was being used the way it was meant to be used.
Near the back of the gallery, in front of a painting Ethan called his favorite without any preamble, they stood shoulder to shoulder.
Close enough that he was aware of her in his peripheral vision.
The painting was small. Unexpected, given its size and the confidence it carried. A figure at a window, back to the viewer, looking out at something the painter had chosen not to show.
He was quiet for a moment.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: Something she's decided about. Whatever it is—she's already decided.
The light catching one edge of the silhouette.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: You think it's good or bad? What she decided?

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: That's what the painting is about. It doesn't matter what's outside. It matters that she's choosing to look.
When he glanced at her, her expression had changed.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I like the way you see things.
Her voice was quieter than usual.
Ethan felt the words settle somewhere he didn't have a name for.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: You already said that. In a different way.
In here, it didn't have to.
